steps.title: Use corresponding angles
steps.content: When two triangles are similar, corresponding angles are equal. The problem states that $\angle F$ corresponds to $\angle J$, so $\angle F = \angle J$.

steps.title: Apply the sine equality
steps.content:
Equal angles have equal sine values, so:
$$\sin(F)=\sin(J)$$
Since $\sin(F)=\frac{308}{317}$, it follows that $\sin(J)=\frac{308}{317}$. That confirms B is correct.
